steps.text: This is a clock! Clocks have numbers all the way around. Can you tap the number 1?
steps.feedback: One! Now tap 2!
Two! Now tap 3!
Three! Now tap 4!
Four! Now tap 5!
Five! Now tap 6 — it's at the very bottom!
Six! Halfway around. Now tap 7!
Seven! Now tap 8!
Eight! Now tap 9!
Nine! Now tap 10!
Ten! Now tap 11!
Eleven! Last one — tap 12 at the very top!
Twelve! You counted all the way around!

steps.text: Some numbers on the clock have special jobs — they point in every direction. Let's find them!
The 12 lives at the very top. Can you tap it?
steps.feedback: 12 at the top — the clock always starts here!

steps.text: Now find the 6 — it's at the bottom!
steps.feedback: 6 at the bottom, right opposite 12.
3 on the right side!
9 on the left. These four numbers point in every direction!

text: You found all four special numbers!
Amazing — you know your way around!

steps.text: The clock has two hands — one long, one short. They tell us the time!
Can you tap the long hand?
steps.feedback: That's the long hand — it counts the minutes.
The short hand tells us the hour — like 1 o'clock or 3 o'clock.

text: Long hand, short hand — you know them both!

steps.text: Different times of day have different names. Let's explore them!
Press Breakfast — what time do you eat breakfast?
steps.feedback: Breakfast time! The short hand is near the 10.
Lunchtime! The short hand has moved around to the 1.
Dinner time — it's getting dark outside!
Bedtime! Time to sleep.

text: Breakfast, lunch, dinner, bedtime — you know your whole day!

steps.text: Let's watch the day go by — from the moment you wake up to when the stars come out!

steps.feedback: Morning! The sun is just coming up.
Noon — the sun is right at the top of the sky!
Afternoon — the sun starts moving down.
Night! The moon comes out and the stars appear.

text: Morning, noon, afternoon, night — you followed the whole day!
